'''Rith Tar'ak''' was an [[Ebranite]] operative for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].

==Biography==
===Invasion of Ebra===
[[Rith Tar'ak]] was an [[Ebranite]] who along with her [[Gender/Legends|sisters]], [[Nyik]] and [[Tor'ara]], were part of the [[Rull]] clan on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ebra]]<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" /> located in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref> Tar'ak eventually became a warrior for clan Rull, one of the few Ebranite clans that still had a warring society during Tar'ak's life.<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" />  

Early during its [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]],<ref name="CSWE Jenton">{{CSWECite|II|156|Jenton, Evitch}}</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] took control of the planet and installed [[Evitch Jenton]] as its Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]].<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" /> After the newly appointed governor instituted martial law,<ref name="CSWE Jenton" /> 

At some point during the occupation, Tar'ak and her sisters were the first to volunteer for an [[Assault on the Imperial facility|assault]] organized by the Rull clan leader agaisnt an Imperial facility on the planet. However the attack force was [[Death/Legends|slaughtered]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]]. The handful of Ebranite survivors, including Tar'ak, Nyik, and Tor'ara were taken [[Prisoner/Legends|captive]] and were forced to harvest [[lu-ramin]], a substance that grew in [[Cave/Legends|caves]] near the Imperial facility.<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" /> 

===Joining the Rebel Alliance===
Soon after the sisters' [[Slavery/Legends|enslavement]], Tar'ak led the group of Ebranites in an escape where they captured an Imperial [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and forced him to fly them off Ebra in a [[starship]]. Soon after, they were captured by a vessel affiliated with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and were given the opportunity to join the Alliance, which they quickly accepted.<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" /> 

The sisters served with the Alliance throughout the [[Kesh sector/Legends|Kesh sector]] which contained Ebra. They eventually formed the [[Sandstorm Unit]], a [[Desert/Legends|desert]] infiltration force under the [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]] [[Scandium Team]]. During its operation, the Sandstorm Unit achieved one of the best records of success of the Scandium units. 

Around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]<ref name="Date">The article "[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)|Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]" in ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10]]'' places the Wellte-ir Massacre before the [[Battle of Thovinack]]. ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' places the Battle of Thovinack shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to 0 BBY.</ref> twenty-one members of the Scandium Team, including Tar'ak and her sisters, fought a four [[Standard day/Legends|day]] battle against two Imperial [[Tank/Legends|Armor]] units on the planet [[Bresnan]] in the [[Wellte-ir system/Legends|Wellte-ir system]]. Tar'ak survived the battle which became known as the [[Wellte-ir Massacre]] and resulted in the death of fifteen members of the Scandium Team including Nyik and Tor'ara.<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" />

===Aftermath===
After the massacre, the Scandium Team being disbanded and Tar'ak was transfered to [[Yntrann]], a terrestrial object<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" /> in the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Yntrann system/Legends|Yntrann system]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Yntrann system/Legends|Yntrann system]]</ref> where she briefly [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] a small yet highly decorated desert [[Task force/Legends|task force]] in the a Special Forces wilderness [[Regiment/Legends|regiment]] for the Alliance. However the leadership position did not appeal to Tar'ak despite her success at it. She eventually turned down a promotion to [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]] and requested a transfer to position with Alliance [[Recruitment Agent|recruitment]].<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" />

Her request was granted and she became one of two individuals tasked with recruitment and training on her homeworld of Ebra. While working as a recruitment agent, Tar'ak captured [[A-5DS]], a [[5DS Ebranite Relations Droid]], which she reprogrammed and later used as a contact among the Ebranite settlements around the [[Grythsorin]] canyon on Ebra. [[Korgath]], Tar'ak's friend and former teammate, eventually began trying to convince her to return to field work despite her reluctance after the massacre.<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" />

==Personality and traits==
Tar'ak was a stocky Ebranite with her clan's insignia on the shoulder of her uppermost left [[Limb/Legends|arm]]. The insignia consisted of a [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Hag'thyyr]] wielding a simple [[Spear/Legends|spear]] while engulfed in [[Fire/Legends|flames]]. Like many Ebranite who served with the Alliance, she was slow to accept the use of high technology except when such items were necessary. She never used [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] preferring to use [[Melee/Legends|melee]] weapons and her [[Kallinn]] [[Bow/Legends|bow]]. She was distrustful of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and was wary of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel. She was eventually able to overcome the painful memories of the Wellte-ir Massacre and continue to fight the Galactic Empire.<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" />

==Skills and abilities==
Trained as a warrior of the clan Rull, Tar'ak was prepared with a number of necessary skills including beast riding, rock climbing and brawling. She was skilled with a Kallinn bow and a [[Untarr]] spear. Tar'ak excelled in leadership and was considered an expert in desert and cavernous survival.<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" />

==Equipment==
While working as a Rebel Alliance recruitment officer Tar'ak used a Kallinn bow with an accompanying quiver and  [[Arrow/Legends|arrows]] as well as a Untarr spear and natural [[Medpac/Legends|medpacs]].<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rith Tar'ak was mentioned in [[1995]] ''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]'', a supplement to ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[West End Games]] and written by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[Harry Heckel]], [[Pablo Hidalgo]], [[Jean Rabe]], [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], [[Lisa Smedman]], and [[Trevor J. Wilson]].<ref name="Adventure Journal 11" />
